slides.title: An example deck
slides.subtitle: Generated by the build-presentation skill as the first-run smoke test

slides.title: What this skill does
bullets: Turns a JSON spec into an editable Google Slides deck
Supports seven slide types — title, section, content, content_image, image, two_column, quote
Renders text in IBM Plex Sans with consistent typography
Uploads embedded images to Drive automatically
slides.speaker_notes: This is a smoke-test deck produced when you first install the build-presentation skill. If you can see this in your Drive, OAuth is working. Replace this spec with your own once you're ready.

slides.title: How you'll use it
left: Write or edit a spec JSON file
Run the Python tool with the spec path
Open the deck URL printed on stdout
right: Don't edit the live deck — changes are lost on regenerate
slides.speaker_notes: The model is edit-spec, re-run. Decks regenerate cheaply. Manual edits to the live deck don't persist across regenerations.

slides.title: That's it — the tool is working
Next steps
bullets: Replace specs/example.json with your real meeting deck
Configure target_folder_id and archive_folder_id if you want decks in a specific Drive folder
Customise the font, colours, and layout in build_presentation.py if your brand differs
slides.speaker_notes: The defaults work for most audiences but every brand has its own palette. The script is short enough to read in one sitting.
